page: Microsoft Azure E64-32s_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the ESv5 server family. It is powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor operating at 2.8 GHz, utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The instance features 32 dedicated vCPUs mapped from 16 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. It is configured with 512.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 16 GB per vCPU. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable, relying on network-attached storage for persistent data.

Benchmark results indicate strong memory subsystem performance, with uncached large-block read bandwidth reaching 205.66 GB/sec and write bandwidth at 116.03 GB/sec, both placing in the top 25% tier. PassMark memory testing also reflects this strong performance profile. Compute benchmarks, including stress-ng single-core (1899.39) and multi-core (31074.98) tests, fall within the average range. The server demonstrates stable processing capabilities for standard operations, such as Redis SET operations at 31.69 million operations per second and static web serving throughput of 37.26 GB/sec. However, large-scale LLM inference is a notable bottleneck, with prompt processing for a 70B model scoring in the bottom 25% at 6.45 tokens per second.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the E64-32s_v5 is structured to optimize memory density relative to compute resources. By offering 512 GB of RAM with 32 vCPUs, it allows organizations to run memory-heavy applications without incurring the costs of licensing or provisioning unnecessary CPU cores. This makes the instance highly suitable for memory-intensive workloads such as in-memory databases, large-scale caching layers, enterprise resource planning (ERP) systems, and data analytics pipelines that require substantial memory footprints but moderate CPU scaling.
description: Microsoft Azure E64-32s_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the ESv5 family, running on Microsoft Hyper-V. It features an Intel Xeon 8370C processor with 32 dedicated vCPUs, 16 physical cores, and 512.0 GB of RAM, providing a high 16 GB per vCPU ratio. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs but supports Premium Storage. In benchmark evaluations, the server exhibits strong memory performance, with large-block read bandwidth reaching 205.66 GB/sec and write bandwidth at 116.03 GB/sec. General compute benchmarks, such as PassMark and Geekbench, align with average tier expectations, while large-scale LLM inference represents a performance tradeoff. This resource configuration delivers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound applications by minimizing unneeded compute overhead. It is best suited for in-memory databases, large caching systems, and enterprise applications requiring substantial memory capacity.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E64-32s_v5 is a memory-optimized VM featuring 32 vCPUs and 512 GB RAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C CPU, it delivers strong memory bandwidth for database and caching workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E64-32s_v5 is a memory-optimized VM with 32 vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and strong memory bandwidth, ideal for databases and caching.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring high RAM-to-vCPU density and strong memory bandwidth for demanding database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8370C processor with 32 dedicated vCPUs and 16 physical cores
512 GB of system memory providing a high 16 GB per vCPU ratio
Strong memory bandwidth performance exceeding 205 GB/sec for large-block reads
Premium Storage capable configuration with no local disk or GPU resources
Optimized for in-memory databases, large caching layers, and enterprise applications
